DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-18-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Courtroom Appearances:
John A. Kithas, Esq. (415-788-8100), appearing for plaintiff Marua Loredo
Brendan F. Macaulay, Esq. (415-398-3600), appearing for defendant All American Financial Group Inc., Eugene Tyler, Babak Broukhim

Courtcall Appearance:
Glenn H. Wechsler, Esq. (925-274-0200) appearing for defendant Option One Mortgage

Ruling:
Prior to the hearing, the Court issues the following tentative ruling: Demurrer is overruled. The fact support causes of action for intentional misrepresentation, concealment and fraud. The complaint alleges that Broukman's liability is largely premised on the agency allegations. The conspiracy allegation is sufficient because the allegation is that it involves all of the defendants, not just corporate employees with the corporation. The decision in Holley v. Crank (9th Cir. 2004) 400 F.3d 667, 671-672 recognizes a private right of action under B&P section 10159.2. The facts alleged support a cause of action under RICO.
Hearing held and matter argued. DEFENDANTS ALL AMERICAN FINANCIAL GROUP INC., EUGENE TYLER AND BABAK BROUKHIM'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff is to link conduct of these defendants. Plaintiff has till 9/5 to amend his complaint. Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600 (302/PJM/AY)
